response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, well-rounded software engineer with strong craftsmanship practices, but the profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. Professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience (MySQL/MongoDB only), and no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets. The mismatch on all core requirements makes this a poor fit despite otherwise solid engineering credentials.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with strong testing/TDD and software-craftsmanship practices
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, caching, high-performance services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Solid CI/CD, DevOps, and cross-platform tooling background
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, dated to 2014-2015, versus the mandatory 5-10 years
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB
No Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong general engineering and systems fundamentals
